title: T-006 — Reference.RolePermissions schema + Apex seed (NPD perms)
prompt:
# T-006 — Reference.RolePermissions schema + Apex seed

PRD: 01-NPD-PRD.md §2.2 + N-U6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create Reference.RolePermissions schema-driven RBAC table and seed Apex base roles with the 19 permissions listed in §2.2 (including risk.write, compliance_doc.write, formulation.create_draft/lock, recipe.submit_for_trial, pilot.promote_to_bom, npd.gate.advance/approve).

## Implementation contract
Migration 0015 emits Reference.RolePermissions(role TEXT, permission TEXT, allowed BOOLEAN, scope_qualifier TEXT, tenant_id UUID, PK(tenant_id,role,permission,scope_qualifier)) + RLS. Seed inserts 6 roles × 19 perms = 114 rows for Apex tenant per §2.2 matrix. Drizzle schema. Helper hasPermission(role, perm, scope?) returning boolean.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0015_role_permissions.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/role-permissions.ts [create]
- packages/db/seeds/role-permissions-apex.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/src/has-permission.ts [create]
- packages/rbac/__tests__/has-permission.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given seed ran, when querying for npd_manager + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=true; for core_user + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=false
2. Given seed ran, when counting permissions for admin role, then all 19 perms present with allowed=true except where matrix says ❌
3. Given hasPermission('technical_manager','compliance_doc.write','dept=technical') called, then returns true

## Test strategy
- RED: 6 unit tests covering matrix corners (npd_manager d365 yes; core_user d365 no; admin schema.edit yes; viewer fa.create no; technical_manager compliance_doc.write yes; npd_manager risk.write yes)
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db seed:role-permissions-apex && pnpm --filter rbac test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not bypass tenant scoping
- Do not hardcode role names in production code (use seed config)
- Do not seed without scope_qualifier where matrix says 'own dept'

pipeline_inputs.description: Create Reference.RolePermissions table + Apex seed of 19 NPD perms × 6 roles per §2.2 matrix.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0015 emits the table with composite PK + RLS. Seed file populates Apex tenant with full matrix. hasPermission helper queries by role+perm+scope. Tests cover 6 representative matrix cells.
out_of_scope: Do not implement MFA flow here (T-022 covers MFA gate for d365_builder.execute)
Do not build admin UI for editing perms
acceptance_criteria: Given seed ran, when querying for npd_manager + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=true; for core_user + d365_builder.execute, then allowed=false
Given seed ran, when counting permissions for admin role, then all 19 perms present with allowed=true except matrix-specified denials
Given hasPermission('technical_manager','compliance_doc.write','dept=technical') called, then it returns true
test_strategy: RED: 6 unit tests covering matrix corners before seed exists
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db seed:role-permissions-apex && pnpm --filter rbac test'
risk_red_lines: Do not bypass tenant scoping in queries
Do not hardcode role names in production code paths
Do not omit scope_qualifier on dept-scoped perms
